questions.q: What programming language does Flutter use?
What is a Widget in Flutter?
options: The basic building block of the UI — everything is a widget

questions.q: What is the difference between StatelessWidget and StatefulWidget?
options: StatelessWidget is immutable; StatefulWidget can change state over time

questions.q: What does the 'build' method do?
options: Describes the UI by returning a widget tree

questions.q: What is the purpose of setState()?
options: Notifies the framework to rebuild the widget with updated state

questions.q: What is a Scaffold widget?
options: A basic material design layout structure with appBar, body, and more

questions.q: What is hot reload in Flutter?
options: Injecting updated code into the running app without losing state

questions.q: What is the difference between hot reload and hot restart?
options: Hot reload preserves state; hot restart resets the entire app state
Hot reload is for web only

questions.q: What is the pubspec.yaml file used for?
options: Defining project metadata, dependencies, and assets

questions.q: What is a MaterialApp widget?
options: The root widget that provides Material Design styling and navigation

questions.q: What is Navigator in Flutter?
options: A widget that manages a stack of Route objects for page navigation

questions.q: What does Navigator.push() do?
options: Adds a new route on top of the navigation stack

questions.q: What is a Future in Dart?
options: An object representing a value that will be available asynchronously

questions.q: What does the 'async' keyword do?
options: Marks a function as asynchronous, allowing the use of await

questions.q: What is a Stream in Dart?
options: A sequence of asynchronous events/data over time

questions.q: What is the Container widget used for?
options: Combining common painting, positioning, and sizing of a child widget

questions.q: What does the Expanded widget do?
options: Makes a child fill the available space in a Row/Column

questions.q: What is the difference between Row and Column?
options: Row arranges children horizontally; Column arranges vertically
Row is for text; Column for images

questions.q: What is InheritedWidget used for?
options: Efficiently propagating data down the widget tree

questions.q: What is Provider in Flutter?
options: A state management solution built on InheritedWidget

questions.q: What does the ListView widget do?
options: Displays a scrollable list of widgets

questions.q: What is the GestureDetector widget?
options: A widget that detects gestures like taps, swipes, and drags

questions.q: What does the FutureBuilder widget do?
options: Builds a widget based on the latest snapshot of a Future's result

questions.q: What is the key property used for?
options: Preserving widget state and identity during rebuilds

questions.q: What is a GlobalKey?
options: A key unique across the entire app, allowing access to widget state from anywhere

questions.q: What is the AnimationController used for?
options: Controlling the duration and playback of animations

questions.q: What does the SingleTickerProviderStateMixin do?
options: Provides a single Ticker for AnimationController in a State class

questions.q: What is the difference between mainAxisAlignment and crossAxisAlignment?
options: main is along the primary direction; cross is perpendicular to it

questions.q: What is a Theme in Flutter?
options: A collection of design properties applied consistently across the app

questions.q: What does MediaQuery provide?
options: Information about the device's screen size, orientation, and display properties

questions.q: What is the Stack widget used for?
options: Overlapping widgets on top of each other with positioning

questions.q: What is the Positioned widget?
options: Controls where a child sits within a Stack using top/left/right/bottom

questions.q: What is dependency injection in Flutter?
options: Providing objects to widgets without hard-coding the creation, often via Provider

questions.q: What does the dispose() method do?
options: Cleans up resources (controllers, listeners) when a StatefulWidget is removed

questions.q: What is the initState() method?
options: Called once when the State is created, used for initializations

questions.q: What is the Bloc pattern?
options: A state management pattern separating business logic from presentation

questions.q: What does the SafeArea widget do?
options: Ensures content avoids system UI intrusions like notches and status bars

questions.q: What is the ClipRRect widget?
options: Clips its child with rounded rectangle corners

questions.q: What is the difference between pushNamed and push?
options: pushNamed uses route names defined in routes table; push takes a Route object directly

questions.q: What is the Wrap widget?
options: Displays children in a flow layout, wrapping to the next line when space runs out

questions.q: What is the showDialog function?
options: Displays a Material Design dialog box over the current content

questions.q: What is the BottomNavigationBar?
options: A Material widget displaying navigation items at the bottom of the screen

questions.q: What is the Hero widget used for?
options: Creating shared element transitions between routes

questions.q: What does the Opacity widget do?
options: Makes its child partially or fully transparent

questions.q: What is the SizedBox widget?
options: A box with a specified width and height, often for spacing

questions.q: What is platform channel in Flutter?
options: A mechanism to call native iOS/Android code from Flutter using message passing

questions.q: What is the difference between final and const in Dart?
options: final is set once at runtime; const is a compile-time constant

questions.q: What is the TextEditingController?
options: A controller for reading and modifying text in a TextField

questions.q: What does the Flexible widget do?
options: Controls how a child of Row/Column flexes to fill available space

questions.q: What is tree shaking in Flutter?
options: Removing unused code during compilation to reduce app size
